description_long: This is a large, four-story apartment building with a steeply pitched, hipped roof covered in reddish-brown shingles. The facade features a half-timbered design with dark wooden beams forming geometric patterns over light plaster walls, giving it a traditional European or Tudor appearance. The lower level is constructed from stone blocks, providing a sturdy base and visual contrast to the upper stories. Windows are symmetrically arranged in rows across each floor, with wooden shutters and frames, and the main entrance is a set of double wooden doors at the center of the ground floor. The roof has a slight overhang with gently upturned eaves, adding to the building's historic character. There are no visible balconies or exterior ornamentation beyond the timber framing.
description: A four-story, half-timbered apartment building with a steep, reddish-brown hipped roof and symmetrical window rows. The stone base and wooden details evoke a traditional European architectural style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main double-door entrance, central window alignment, and prominent half-timbered facade.
description_view_1: Side view with symmetrical window placement and the same half-timbered pattern, but no visible entrance.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, but without a visible entrance, maintaining the same window and timber arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, identical to view 1, with symmetrical windows and timber framing.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are symmetrically arranged in evenly spaced rows across each floor, with one window per section between timber beams, creating a regular grid pattern.
